# Name: # OTA-capable layout on 4 MB flash. Two equal app slots (ota_0/ota_1)
# of 1664 KB each — sized to fit the WiFi-enabled build (~1.4 MB)
# with ~15 % headroom. Earlier: 1472 KB (4 % headroom
# 1280 KB (overflowed when the Ethernet default flipped to WiFi).
# Storage partition keeps the "storage" label so announcement.c
# continues to mount it; size dropped from 1024 KB to 640 KB after
# realising the user-recorded announcement is capped at 240 KB
# (see ANNOUNCEMENT_MAX_BYTES in announcement.h) — 640 KB still
# leaves ~2.7× headroom over the cap
# and any future small assets.
# The 4 KB at 0x12000 (right after phy_init) is left as an unused gap.
# It formerly held a per-install "pairing" partition (custom data
# subtype 0x40) that the browser flasher wrote with a random secret so
# the install page could locate a freshly-flashed dongle on the LAN.
# That handshake was dropped in favour of plain mDNS host-name lookup
# (issue #318); dongles flashed with the old layout keep the now-unused
# partition until they are reflashed over USB.
# The "coredump" partition (52 KB) sits in the alignment gap before
# ota_0 (which must start at the next 64 KB boundary
# ESP32 panic handler writes an ELF-formatted core dump there on crash;
# on the next boot
Type: crashreport.c uploads it to the PhoneBlock backend

# Name: # and erases the slot. Dongles flashed with the older layout don't have
# this partition — esp_core_dump_image_check returns ESP_ERR_NOT_FOUND
# there and the upload path is skipped.
# One-way migration: existing dongles running the old factory-only
# layout must be reflashed over USB once. From there on
# flip between ota_0 and ota_1 automatically.
nvs
otadata
phy_init
coredump
ota_0
ota_1
storage
